channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Alycia Debnam-Carey is Single: Details on Her Past Rumours and Dating History
item.description: Alycia Debnam-Carey, the talented Australian actress known for her roles in popular TV shows like Fear the Walking Dead and The 100, is currently single as of 2026. She prefers to keep her personal li...

item.title: Government launches seven-point plan to drive industrialisation and support youth entrepreneurs
item.description: Tanzania government imeweka mkakati wa pointi saba wenye lengo la kuharakisha ukuaji wa viwanda, kuongeza ushindani wa biashara na kutoa nafasi kwa wabunifu wadogo wadogo na vijana. Waziri wa Viwanda ...

item.title: WHO increases Ebola alert in DRC amid rising suspected cases and reports hantavirus outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organisation (WHO) don raise the Ebola outbreak risk level for Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) to very high within the country as suspected cases near 750, with the disease don als...

item.title: Historic Persephone Boat from The Beachcombers Successfully Restored in Gibsons
item.description: The iconic red-and-black Persephone boat, featured in the long-running Canadian television series The Beachcombers, has been fully restored by the community of Gibsons, British Columbia. Originally na...

item.title: B.C. Conservative MP Welcomes Grandson Charlie Kirk Jansen
item.description: A Member of Parliament from British Columbia representing the Conservative Party has announced the birth of his grandson, Charlie Kirk Jansen. The MP shared the news publicly, expressing joy and pride...

item.title: Dr Ofweneke Biography: Kenyan Comedian Reveals Real Name Sande Bush Oyolo, Luhya Tribe Roots and Schooling
item.description: Dr Ofweneke, whose real name is Sande Bush Oyolo, is a popular Kenyan comedian, TV and radio host, actor and motivational speaker born on 17 January 1991 at Pumwani Hospital in Nairobi. The funnyman i...

item.title: Mother convicted of murdering her two children through poisoning in Johannesburg park
item.description: A 43-year-old Zimbabwean woman, Lister Nkomo, has been found guilty in the Johannesburg Magistrates’ Court for the murder of her two young children, following an incident at Empire Park in Parktown ne...

item.title: Analysing the impact of immigration on Australia’s housing market
item.description: The debate around immigration and its effect on housing affordability in Australia continues to capture public attention. Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has raised concerns suggesting that migrants co...

item.title: Momina Iqbal files complaint at Chung Police Station against PML-N MPA Saqib Chadhar for harassment, blackmail and threats
item.description: Renowned Pakistani actor and model Momina Iqbal has approached the Chung Police Station in Lahore to register an FIR against PML-N MPA Saqib Chadhar. She has levelled serious allegations including bla...

item.title: Congressman Tom Kean Jr. absent from votes while continuing travel and stock activities
item.description: Rep. Tom Kean Jr. (R-NJ), a Republican congressman, has been absent from the House since early March 2026, missing 88 votes due to reported personal medical issues. Despite this, recent reports indica...

item.title: 18 Female Police Recruits Seek Court Intervention After Expulsion Over Pregnancy Allegations
item.description: Eighteen female recruits at the National Police College in Kiganjo have approached the High Court after being expelled over positive pregnancy tests barely a month into training. According to the peti...

item.title: BMONI and Mastercard launch instant virtual and physical cards for Nigerian users
item.description: BMONI, one AI-powered financial platform wey dey make am easy for people to manage money, don join hand with Mastercard to introduce new virtual and physical cards for Nigerians. This new card system ...

item.title: Karnataka requests Centre to raise ESI ceiling following minimum wage hike
item.description: The Karnataka government has formally urged the Central Government to revise the Employees’ State Insurance (ESI) ceiling from ₹21,000 per month to ₹30,000, following a substantial increase in minimum...

item.title: President Samia Suluhu Hassan appoints new chairs for government boards including PSSSF
item.description: President Samia Suluhu Hassan has made important appointments for three government boards in Tanzania. This includes naming new chairpersons to lead key institutions. According to the statement from C...

item.title: PNP confirms 11 deaths in Toboso clash had gunpowder traces
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) reported that 11 out of 19 people killed in a series of encounters in Barangay Salamanca, Toboso, Negros Occidental last April tested positive for gunpowder. This ...

item.title: Man Critically Injured in California Attack Near Pro-Trump Display; Suspect Reportedly Mentally Ill Navy Veteran
item.description: In Escondido, California, 69-year-old Kerry Sheron, known for his prominent pro-Trump home display, was critically injured in an assault near his residence. Police responded to the scene Wednesday aft...

item.title: Exploring Talent: Natural Gift Versus Years of Dedication in Sport
item.description: Justin Langer reflects on the nature of talent in sport, drawing on his 35 years of professional experience. He argues that talent is often misunderstood, with some believing it is purely innate, whil...

item.title: Albino Buffalo in Bangladesh Attracts Crowds Ahead of Eid Festival
item.description: A rare albino buffalo in Bangladesh has captured the attention of locals and visitors alike ahead of the Eid al-Adha festival. Known for its flowing blond hair and striking resemblance to former US Pr...

item.title: Big Oil Companies Renew Interest in Alaska Oil and Gas Projects Due to Middle East Crisis
item.description: Dis article talk about how big oil companies don begin to show fresh interest in drilling for oil and gas for Alaska, one place wey dem bin dey avoid before. Because of di crisis for Middle East wey d...

item.title: Study Shows Traffic Significantly Contributes to Urban Heat
item.description: Recent research highlights that urban traffic is a notable contributor to the rising temperatures in cities, a phenomenon often overlooked in urban planning. Known as the urban heat island effect, cit...
